zh.lifeSummary: 當Hamon I de Massey 1st Baron of Dunham於1056年出生在法國巴-諾門迪奧內時，他的父親Guillaume de la Ferte-Mace21歲，母親Muriel de Conteville27歲。他於1098年與Margaret de Sacey在英國爾鄧翰 梅西結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1118年在英國爾包頓享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特武德內斯波洛聖 瑪麗 馮 維金 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).
English: variant of Marsay (see Marcey ).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeen): variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Marsee
meanings: English: altered form of   Massey  .

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Germain.
Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .
Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Masey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Macy
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

nameMeanings.name: Hamon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Raimundo . See Raymond .

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Hamon I de Massey 1st Baron of Dunham was born in 1056, in Orne, Lower Normandy, France, his father, Guillaume de la Ferte-Mace, was 21 and his mother, Muriel de Conteville, was 27. He married Margaret de Sacey in 1098, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1118, in Bowdon, Cheshire, England, at the age of 62, and was buried in St. Mary The Virgin Churchyard, Woodnesborough, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Margaret de Sacey en 1098, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1118, à Bowdon, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à St. Mary The Virgin Churchyard, Woodnesborough, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hamon I de Massey 1st Baron of Dunhamが1056年にフランスバセ-ノーマンディオルンで生まれた時，父Guillaume de la Ferte-Maceは21歳，母Muriel de Contevilleは27歳であった。1098年にイギリスチェシュアイアダンハム マセイでMargaret de Saceyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1118年にイギリスチェシュアイアボウドンで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントウッドネスボロウスト. マリー ス バージン チャーチヤルドにて埋葬された。

ko.St Mary the Virgin Churchyard, Cheshire,: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 우드네스버루 스트. 메리 테 버긴 처르c히아어드
ko.lifeSummary: Hamon I de Massey 1st Baron of Dunham(이)가 1056년 프랑스 배세-노먼디 른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guillaume de la Ferte-Mace(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Muriel de Conteville(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1098년 영국 체실르 던함 마시에서 Margaret de Sacey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1118년 영국 체실르 보우던에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 우드네스버루 스트. 메리 테 버긴 처르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hamon I de Massey 1st Baron of Dunham nasceu em 1056, em Orne, Basse-Normandie, França, seu pai, Guillaume de la Ferte-Mace, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Muriel de Conteville, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret de Sacey em 1098, em Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1118, em Bowdon, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em St. Mary The Virgin Churchyard, Woodnesborough, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St Mary the Virgin Churchyard, Cheshire,: ст. мэри т виргин чурчярд, вуднесбороую, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hamon I de Massey 1st Baron of Dunham родился в 1056 г. в местности орн, бассе-норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Guillaume de la Ferte-Mace было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Muriel de Conteville, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret de Sacey в 1098 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1118 г. в местности боудон, чешир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности ст. мэри т виргин чурчярд, вуднесбороую, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hamon I de Massey 1st Baron of Dunham nació en 1056, en Orne, Basse-Normandie, Francia, su papá, Guillaume de la Ferte-Mace, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Muriel de Conteville, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Margaret de Sacey en 1098, en Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1118, en Bowdon, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en St. Mary The Virgin Churchyard, Woodnesborough, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
